notes:
IFC-311 wires the two notifyOnOwnerChange Cat-1 toggles that PG-182 + PG-183 shipped but left dormant. Four new tRPC mutations added (account.reassign, account.bulkReassign, contact.reassign, contact.bulkReassign), one new validator entry (account_reassigned), and the legacy account.assignOwner refactored to delegate to performAccountReassign — preserving the AccountDetail.tsx:142 contract while gaining the previously-missing notification wiring. Helpers extracted to dedicated account-reassign.ts + contact-reassign.ts modules for clean coverage scoping (97.46/88.88/100/100). The stale `IFC-311 reassign endpoint` reference in deal-automation.ts:24-31 corrected to clarify that deal owner-change is a separate follow-up (out of CSV scope). Plan-reviewer subagent (id a41bd0effe4183ee0) returned CHANGE-REQUIRED with 3 actionable findings — all applied before exec started.

POST-COMPLETION REVIEW (2026-04-21) — three findings + one open question, all addressed:
  • Finding 1 (medium, dead-on-arrival contact UI wiring) — concrete follow-up filed as PG-210 in Sprint_plan.csv row 590, target sprint 19, with anti-spam-checked sidecar at .specify/sprints/sprint-18/follow-ups/IFC-311-follow-ups.json. Spec Non-Goals updated to cite PG-210. **Scope broadened 2026-04-21** after a user-triggered audit revealed that account.bulkReassign is also fully orphaned (no UI caller) and AccountDetail.tsx:152 still invokes the legacy assignOwner name rather than the canonical account.reassign. PG-210 now covers all four new procedures (contact.reassign, contact.bulkReassign, account.reassign, account.bulkReassign) plus the cosmetic AccountDetail.tsx migration. Account side's single-reassign notification path IS reachable today via the assignOwner→performAccountReassign alias (validated runtime), but bulk-reassign on accounts and both contact procedures wait on PG-210 before they can be exercised by real users.
  • Finding 2 (low, $transaction over-scoped) — applied: performAccountReassign + performContactReassign now run findFirst + authz + idempotency OUTSIDE $transaction, opening tx only for the target-user check + updateMany write path. Saves N wasted BEGIN;COMMIT round-trips for skip/deny rows in bulkReassign. New file hashes captured.
  • Finding 3 (low, misleading audit on TARGET_USER_NOT_FOUND pre-validation) — applied: removed logXxxReassignPermissionDenied call from bulk pre-validation in both routers. Per-row case retained (correct semantics). The TRPCError thrown is the surface for the lookup miss; no audit row claiming `resource=account, id=<user-uuid>` is written.
  • Open Q2 (assignOwner self-reassign audit drift) — resolved by preserving pre-IFC-311 audit behavior: SKIPPED branch in assignOwner now emits a same-state audit log entry (beforeState/afterState both = currentOwnerId). The new reassign / bulkReassign procedures intentionally do NOT emit this audit because their `skipped: true` response field communicates the no-op explicitly to new callers. Two new regression tests added (AC-AO4 + AC-AO4b) lock the behavior so future refactors cannot drift it again. **Follow-up strengthen (2026-04-21)**: AC-AO4 upgraded from a does-not-throw smoke check to a positive-assertion via `vi.spyOn(auditLoggerModule, getAuditLogger)` module-level spy. The test now captures the logAction call BEFORE the audit writer synthetic-tenant short-circuit, asserts `beforeState={ownerId:user1}, afterState={ownerId:user1}`, and will FAIL if the SKIPPED audit branch is removed. AC-AO4b strengthened symmetrically to assert `logAction` is NOT called for the new reassign procedure SKIPPED path. Both tests pass (30/30 in file, 106/106 in full account suite).

Post-review re-validation: 130/130 tests pass (52 reassign + 76 legacy router + 2 new self-reassign cases); scoped coverage holds at 97.46/88.88/100/100; lint and typecheck exit 0 on all 5 changed files.

Pre-existing IFC-310 in-flight typecheck/SonarJS warnings in unrelated files (contact.router.ts create/update procedures, duplicate-rule-evaluator.ts, etc.) remain — those are owned by IFC-310, NOT IFC-311; documented in delivery report. No Lighthouse KPI in scope (backend-only task). No new page.tsx (Gate 12 N/A). Architecture compliance: API-layer only, no leak into packages/domain or packages/application.
